content.text:
Excellent! Now I have all the information needed. Let me compile a comprehensive report for the user.

## Comprehensive Analysis Report

I've read both `common/chat.h` and `common/chat.cpp` completely. Here are all the relevant details you requested:

### 1. **common_chat_format Enum Values** (from chat.h:147-156)

```
COMMON_CHAT_FORMAT_CONTENT_ONLY       // Default: just text content, no special parsing
COMMON_CHAT_FORMAT_PEG_SIMPLE         // PEG parser for simple format
COMMON_CHAT_FORMAT_PEG_NATIVE         // PEG parser for native format (used by all specialized handlers)
COMMON_CHAT_FORMAT_PEG_GEMMA4         // Special Gemma4 format
COMMON_CHAT_FORMAT_COUNT              // Not a format, just the count
```

### 2. **Specialized Template Handler Functions - Signature & Common Pattern**

All specialized template handlers follow this signature:
```cpp
static common_chat_params FUNCTION_NAME(
    const common_chat_template & tmpl,
    const autoparser::generation_params & inputs)
```

**Common structure they all follow:**
1. Create and initialize a `common_chat_params data` object
2. Generate a prompt via `common_chat_template_direct_apply_impl(tmpl, inputs, /* optional overrides */)`
3. Set `data.format = COMMON_CHAT_FORMAT_PEG_NATIVE`
4. Optionally set `data.supports_thinking` and `data.thinking_start_tag`/`data.thinking_end_tag`
5. Set `data.preserved_tokens` (list of special tokens not to be tokenized)
6. Build a PEG parser using `build_chat_peg_parser([&](common_chat_peg_builder & p) { ... })`
7. Save parser result to `data.parser`
8. Optionally build grammar if tools are present via `build_grammar([&](const common_grammar_builder & builder) { ... })`
9. Set `data.grammar_triggers` to specify when grammar generation starts
10. Return the fully configured `data` object

### 3. **Existing Specialized Template Handlers** (7 total)

**File location:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775315292816-wbbi4d/common/chat.cpp`

1. **`common_chat_params_init_ministral_3`** (line 825-940)
   - Models: Mistral Large 3, Ministral-3-14B-Reasoning
   - Detection: `[SYSTEM_PROMPT]`, `[TOOL_CALLS]`, `[ARGS]` present, but `[CALL_ID]` absent
   - Thinking support: YES (`[THINK]`/`[/THINK]`)
   - Tool format: Prefix-based with special tokens
   - Preserved tokens: `[THINK]`, `[/THINK]`, `[TOOL_CALLS]`, `[ARGS]`

2. **`common_chat_params_init_gpt_oss`** (line 942-1079)
   - Models: GPT-OSS family
   - Detection: `<|channel|>` token present
   - Thinking support: YES
   - Tool format: Channel-based with `<|channel|>`, `<|message|>`, `<|start|>`, `<|end|>`
   - Special: Maps `reasoning_content` → `thinking` field, handles return/end token replacement
   - Preserved tokens: `<|channel|>`, `<|constrain|>`, `<|message|>`, `<|start|>`, `<|end|>`

3. **`common_chat_params_init_functionary_v3_2`** (line 1081-1171)
   - Models: Functionary v3.2
   - Detection: `>>>all` and `>>>${recipient}` present
   - Thinking support: NO
   - Tool format: Recipient-based `>>>function_name\n{json_args}`
   - Preserved tokens: `>>>all`
   - Grammar trigger: Pattern-based `>>>(?!all)`

4. **`common_chat_params_init_kimi_k2`** (line 1175-1290)
   - Models: Kimi K2 Thinking
   - Detection: `<|tool_calls_section_begin|>` and `<|tool_call_begin|>` present
   - Thinking support: YES (`<think>`/`</think>`)
   - Tool format: ID format `functions.<name>:<index>` with explicit section markers
   - Preserved tokens: Multiple section/call markers + think tags
   - Grammar trigger: Word-based `<|tool_call_begin|>`

5. **`common_chat_params_init_lfm2`** (line 1298-1369)
   - Models: LFM2 family
   - Detection: `<|tool_list_start|>` and `<|tool_list_end|>` present
   - Thinking support: YES (`<think>`/`</think>`)
   - Tool format: Python-style `[function_name(arg="value")]` wrapped in `<|tool_call_start|>...<|tool_call_end|>`
   - Preserved tokens: Multiple tool markers + think tags
   - Grammar trigger: Word-based `<|tool_call_start|>`

6. **`common_chat_params_init_lfm2_5`** (line 1377-1444)
   - Models: LFM2.5 family
   - Detection: `List of tools: [` present but `<|tool_list_start|>` absent
   - Thinking support: YES (`<think>`/`</think>`)
   - Tool format: Bare Python-style with optional `<|tool_call_start|>` prefix
   - Grammar triggers: Per-tool dynamic based on function name `[function_name(`

7. **`common_chat_params_init_gigachat_v3`** (line 1446-1517)
   - Models: GigaChatV3
   - Detection: `<|role_sep|>` and `<|message_sep|>` present, but NOT `<|function_call|>`
   - Thinking support: NO
   - Tool format: JSON structure within special message separator markers
   - Preserved tokens: `<|message_sep|>\n\n`, `<|role_sep|>\n`
   - Grammar trigger: Word-based tool call prefix

### 4. **`common_chat_try_specialized_template()` Function** (line 1634-1691)

**Location:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775315292816-wbbi4d/common/chat.cpp:1634`

**Signature:**
```cpp
std::optional<common_chat_params> common_chat_try_specialized_template(
    const common_chat_template & tmpl,
    const std::string & src,
    const autoparser::generation_params & params)
```

**How it works:**
- Takes the template source string (`src`)
- Performs string-based detection using `src.find()` operations on unique marker tokens
- Returns `std::optional<common_chat_params>` - has value if matched, `std::nullopt` otherwise
- Tests templates in order (first match wins):
  1. Ministral/Magistral Large 3: `[SYSTEM_PROMPT]` AND `[TOOL_CALLS]` AND `[ARGS]` AND NOT `[CALL_ID]`
  2. GPT-OSS: `<|channel|>`
  3. Functionary v3.2: `>>>all` AND `>>>${recipient}`
  4. Kimi K2: `<|tool_calls_section_begin|>` AND `<|tool_call_begin|>`
  5. LFM2: `<|tool_list_start|>` AND `<|tool_list_end|>`
  6. LFM2.5: `List of tools: [` AND NOT `<|tool_list_start|>`
  7. GigaChatV3: `<|role_sep|>` AND `<|message_sep|>` AND NOT `<|function_call|>`

### 5. **How Template Application Works**

**Entry point:** `common_chat_templates_apply()` (line 1872-1876)

Calls either:
- `common_chat_templates_apply_jinja()` (line 1693+) - the main flow
  - Builds autoparser params from inputs
  - Calls `common_chat_try_specialized_template()` to detect if specialized handler needed
  - If specialized: returns custom `common_chat_params`
  - If not specialized: falls back to generic Jinja template handling

**Template Direct Apply:** `common_chat_template_direct_apply_impl()` (line 767-817)
- Renders Jinja template with context containing messages, tools, generation params
- Executes template program against context
- Handles BOS/EOS token management
- **Key inputs to Jinja context:**
  - `messages` (or overrides)
  - `bos_token`, `eos_token`
  - `enable_thinking`
  - `tools` (if present)
  - `add_generation_prompt` flag
  - `extra_context` from inputs

### 6. **Key Data Structures**

**`common_chat_params`** (header lines 177-190):
```cpp
common_chat_format format = COMMON_CHAT_FORMAT_CONTENT_ONLY;
std::string prompt;
std::string grammar;
bool grammar_lazy;
std::string generation_prompt;
bool supports_thinking;
std::string thinking_start_tag;
std::string thinking_end_tag;
std::vector<common_grammar_trigger> grammar_triggers;
std::vector<std::string> preserved_tokens;
std::vector<std::string> additional_stops;
std::string parser;  // Serialized PEG parser
```

**`common_chat_templates_inputs`** (header lines 158-175):
- Contains: messages, grammar, json_schema, tools, tool_choice, parallel_tool_calls
- reasoning_format, enable_thinking, now (timestamp)
- chat_template_kwargs, add_bos, add_eos, force_pure_content

File paths are absolute paths as requested.

static common_chat_params common_chat_params_init_mistral_instruct_v3(
    const common_chat_template &          tmpl,
    const autoparser::generation_params & inputs)
```

The handler:
- Iterates over `inputs.messages` using `msg.value("role", "")` / `msg.value("content", "")`
- Buffers any `system` role message in `pending_system`
- On the **first** `user` turn: prepends `tmpl.bos_token()` (e.g. `<s>`), then inlines the buffered system content (if any) as `{system}\n` before the user text
- Wraps every `user` turn: `[INST] {content} [/INST]`
- Appends every `assistant` turn: ` {content}</s>`
- Sets `data.format = COMMON_CHAT_FORMAT_CONTENT_ONLY` and returns

This produces the canonical Mistral v0.3 format:
```
<s>[INST] {system}\n{user1} [/INST] {assistant1}</s>[INST] {user2} [/INST]
```

**2. Detection entry** (lines 1684–1690), in `common_chat_try_specialized_template()`, placed after the Ministral-3 check:

```cpp
// Mistral Instruct v0.3 - uses [INST]/[/INST] markers without [SYSTEM_PROMPT] special token
if (src.find("[INST]") != std::string::npos && src.find("[/INST]") != std::string::npos &&
    src.find("[SYSTEM_PROMPT]") == std::string::npos) {
    LOG_DBG("Using specialized template: Mistral Instruct v3\n");
    return common_chat_params_init_mistral_instruct_v3(tmpl, params);
}
```

The `[SYSTEM_PROMPT]` absence check cleanly distinguishes Mistral v0.3 from the existing Ministral-3 handler (which requires `[SYSTEM_PROMPT]`). No existing handlers were modified.
